Publication Ready Graphs and Tables in R (SDDRC)

  • Tables for Models

On this page

  • Introduction to gtsummary
  • Descriptive statistics
  • Statistical tests
  • Additional resources

Beautiful Tables for Models

Introduction to gtsummary

Next, we’ll use the gtsummary package, an extension of gt that can incorporate statistical model outputs into publication-ready tables.

Descriptive statistics

The base R approach to getting summary statistics for a dataframe results in a thorough, but not well-formatted for publication, table.

summary(smartpill)
     Group            Gender            Race          Height     
 Min.   :0.0000   Min.   :0.0000   Min.   :1.00   Min.   :132.1  
 1st Qu.:1.0000   1st Qu.:0.0000   1st Qu.:1.00   1st Qu.:164.5  
 Median :1.0000   Median :1.0000   Median :1.00   Median :175.3  
 Mean   :0.9158   Mean   :0.5684   Mean   :1.46   Mean   :172.0  
 3rd Qu.:1.0000   3rd Qu.:1.0000   3rd Qu.:2.00   3rd Qu.:180.3  
 Max.   :1.0000   Max.   :1.0000   Max.   :5.00   Max.   :193.0  
                                   NA's   :8                     
     Weight            Age           GE.Time          SB.Time      
 Min.   : 44.91   Min.   :18.00   Min.   : 1.680   Min.   : 1.810  
 1st Qu.: 66.68   1st Qu.:28.00   1st Qu.: 2.540   1st Qu.: 3.220  
 Median : 74.84   Median :37.00   Median : 3.150   Median : 3.775  
 Mean   : 77.47   Mean   :37.48   Mean   : 5.462   Mean   : 4.297  
 3rd Qu.: 86.18   3rd Qu.:44.00   3rd Qu.: 4.120   3rd Qu.: 4.850  
 Max.   :127.01   Max.   :72.00   Max.   :74.300   Max.   :13.800  
                                  NA's   :3        NA's   :5       
     C.Time          WG.Time       S.Contractions   S.Sum.of.Amplitudes
 Min.   :  0.70   Min.   :  5.99   Min.   :  47.0   Min.   :  655.6    
 1st Qu.: 15.24   1st Qu.: 22.42   1st Qu.: 115.0   1st Qu.: 2648.2    
 Median : 21.66   Median : 30.05   Median : 179.0   Median : 4141.0    
 Mean   : 27.51   Mean   : 58.13   Mean   : 260.1   Mean   : 5548.1    
 3rd Qu.: 37.18   3rd Qu.: 51.22   3rd Qu.: 305.0   3rd Qu.: 6237.8    
 Max.   :118.87   Max.   :816.00   Max.   :1665.0   Max.   :33800.3    
 NA's   :13       NA's   :1        NA's   :14       NA's   :14         
 S.Mean.Peak.Amplitude   S.Mean.pH     SB.Contractions  SB.Sum.of.Amplitudes
 Min.   : 4.553        Min.   :1.470   Min.   : 223.0   Min.   : 3899       
 1st Qu.:19.436        1st Qu.:2.380   1st Qu.: 468.0   1st Qu.: 7848       
 Median :21.277        Median :2.960   Median : 715.0   Median :13305       
 Mean   :22.838        Mean   :3.023   Mean   : 734.9   Mean   :13878       
 3rd Qu.:25.021        3rd Qu.:3.600   3rd Qu.: 917.5   3rd Qu.:18392       
 Max.   :43.439        Max.   :5.930   Max.   :2375.0   Max.   :41123       
 NA's   :14            NA's   :14      NA's   :16       NA's   :16          
 SB.Mean.Peak.Amplitude   SB.Mean.pH    Colon.Contractions
 Min.   :14.98          Min.   :4.720   Min.   :  41.0    
 1st Qu.:16.78          1st Qu.:6.815   1st Qu.: 290.5    
 Median :18.29          Median :7.040   Median : 598.0    
 Mean   :18.75          Mean   :6.980   Mean   : 688.2    
 3rd Qu.:20.10          3rd Qu.:7.230   3rd Qu.: 894.5    
 Max.   :27.85          Max.   :8.550   Max.   :2672.0    
 NA's   :16             NA's   :16      NA's   :16        
 Colon.Sum.of.Amplitudes C.Mean.Peak.Amplitude   C.Mean.pH    
 Min.   :  1873          Min.   :32.82         Min.   :3.920  
 1st Qu.: 12391          1st Qu.:38.50         1st Qu.:6.665  
 Median : 24832          Median :41.15         Median :7.070  
 Mean   : 30126          Mean   :42.71         Mean   :6.919  
 3rd Qu.: 40168          3rd Qu.:45.50         3rd Qu.:7.315  
 Max.   :117708          Max.   :64.22         Max.   :8.100  
 NA's   :16              NA's   :16            NA's   :16

The equivalent summary statistics function from gtsummary is tbl_summary().

tbl_summary(smartpill)
Characteristic N = 951
Group 87 (92%)
Gender 54 (57%)
Race
    1 64 (74%)
    2 12 (14%)
    3 6 (6.9%)
    4 4 (4.6%)
    5 1 (1.1%)
    Unknown 8
Height 175 (164, 180)
Weight 75 (66, 86)
Age 37 (28, 44)
GE.Time 3.2 (2.5, 4.1)
    Unknown 3
SB.Time 3.78 (3.21, 4.85)
    Unknown 5
C.Time 22 (15, 37)
    Unknown 13
WG.Time 30 (22, 51)
    Unknown 1
S.Contractions 179 (115, 305)
    Unknown 14
S.Sum.of.Amplitudes 4,141 (2,648, 6,238)
    Unknown 14
S.Mean.Peak.Amplitude 21.3 (19.4, 25.0)
    Unknown 14
S.Mean.pH 2.96 (2.38, 3.60)
    Unknown 14
SB.Contractions 715 (454, 923)
    Unknown 16
SB.Sum.of.Amplitudes 13,305 (7,639, 18,496)
    Unknown 16
SB.Mean.Peak.Amplitude 18.29 (16.76, 20.14)
    Unknown 16
SB.Mean.pH 7.04 (6.80, 7.23)
    Unknown 16
Colon.Contractions 598 (289, 919)
    Unknown 16
Colon.Sum.of.Amplitudes 24,832 (12,110, 40,580)
    Unknown 16
C.Mean.Peak.Amplitude 41 (38, 46)
    Unknown 16
C.Mean.pH 7.07 (6.66, 7.33)
    Unknown 16
1 n (%); Median (Q1, Q3)

The default summary statistic presented depends on variable type. Categorical variables get counts and percentages while continuous variables get median(first quartile, third quartile). Any NA values are listed in a separate row as “unknown”.

These defaults are customizable. For instance, if we wanted only a subset of variables:

smartpill |>  
  tbl_summary(
    include = c(Group, Age, SB.Time, SB.Contractions)
  )
Characteristic N = 951
Group 87 (92%)
Age 37 (28, 44)
SB.Time 3.78 (3.21, 4.85)
    Unknown 5
SB.Contractions 715 (454, 923)
    Unknown 16
1 n (%); Median (Q1, Q3)

We can incorporate other R functions before using tbl_summary() to convert any unintentionally numeric variables to categories so they are displayed in the table as distinct groups.

smartpill |>  
  mutate(Group = as.factor(Group)) |>
  tbl_summary(
    include = c(Group, Age, SB.Time, SB.Contractions)
  )
Characteristic N = 951
Group
    0 8 (8.4%)
    1 87 (92%)
Age 37 (28, 44)
SB.Time 3.78 (3.21, 4.85)
    Unknown 5
SB.Contractions 715 (454, 923)
    Unknown 16
1 n (%); Median (Q1, Q3)

The default summary statistic presented depends on variable type. Categorical variables get counts and percentages while continuous variables get median(first quartile, third quartile). Any NA values are listed in a separate row as “unknown”.

These defaults are customizable. For instance, if we wanted to display continuous variables as minimum, median, and maximum:

smartpill |>  
  mutate(Group = as.factor(Group)) |>
  tbl_summary(
    include = c(Group, Age, SB.Time, SB.Contractions), 
    statistic = list(
      #change just way continuous vars are presented
      all_continuous() ~ "{median} ({min}, {max})"
    )
  )
Characteristic N = 951
Group
    0 8 (8.4%)
    1 87 (92%)
Age 37 (18, 72)
SB.Time 3.78 (1.81, 13.80)
    Unknown 5
SB.Contractions 715 (223, 2,375)
    Unknown 16
1 n (%); Median (Min, Max)

This table can also be customized with better labels and updated NA indicator:

smartpill |>  
  mutate(Group = as.factor(Group)) |>
  tbl_summary(
    include = c(Group, Age, SB.Time, SB.Contractions), 
    statistic = list(
      #change just way continuous vars are presented
      all_continuous() ~ "{median} ({min}, {max})"
    ),
    label = list(SB.Time = "Small bowel transit time (hours)", SB.Contractions = "Small bowel contractions"),
    missing_text = "Missing"
  )
Characteristic N = 951
Group
    0 8 (8.4%)
    1 87 (92%)
Age 37 (18, 72)
Small bowel transit time (hours) 3.78 (1.81, 13.80)
    Missing 5
Small bowel contractions 715 (223, 2,375)
    Missing 16
1 n (%); Median (Min, Max)

Finally, this can be extended to produce crosstab tables, for instance with descriptive statistics for selected variables for each group

smartpill |>  
  mutate(Group = as.factor(Group)) |>
  tbl_summary(
    by = Group,
    include = c(Age, SB.Time, SB.Contractions), 
    statistic = list(
      #change just way continuous vars are presented
      all_continuous() ~ "{median} ({min}, {max})"
    ),
    label = list(SB.Time = "Small bowel transit time (hours)", SB.Contractions = "Small bowel contractions"),
    missing_text = "Missing"
  )
The following warnings were returned during `tbl_summary()`:
! For variable `SB.Contractions` (`Group = "0"`) and "min" statistic: no
  non-missing arguments to min; returning Inf
! For variable `SB.Contractions` (`Group = "0"`) and "max" statistic: no
  non-missing arguments to max; returning -Inf
Characteristic 0
N = 81		 1
N = 871
Age 41 (23, 57) 35 (18, 72)
Small bowel transit time (hours) 6.70 (3.40, 13.80) 3.75 (1.81, 13.45)
    Missing 1 4
Small bowel contractions NA (Inf, -Inf) 715 (223, 2,375)
    Missing 8 8
1 Median (Min, Max)

Note that warnings will be returned if the metrics are not workable, for instance if all instances in a group are not missing.

Statistical tests

If we wanted to run statistical analyses on this data, a usual approach would be to use a separate R package to run the test and then extract values of interest, like coefficients and p-values, to put into a table. gtsummary streamlines this process with an all-in-one approach to formatting statistical test outputs into a table.

There are many standard statistical tests integrated into gtsummary including t-test, ANOVA, chi-square, regression, survey sample methods, and more.

Let’s look at a couple examples in practice.

Additional resources

  • Primary website for gtsummary including how to cite use of the package.
  • gtsummary reference manual with complete details on functions and arguments.
  • Examples of gtsummary in practice from R Graph Gallery.
